# Slash Commands (source vs rendered)

Covers the four slash commands (`/loop-new`, `/loop-run`, `/loop-resume`, `/loop-status`) â€” their single canonical source in `command/` and the per-host rendered artifacts under `.opencode/command/` and `.claude/commands/`.

## Core Invariants

- `command/` is the ONE source of truth; `.opencode/command/*.md` and `.claude/commands/*.md` are RENDERED artifacts and must never be hand-edited [source: command/AGENTS.md:3, AGENTS.md:50].
- Command bodies in `command/<id>.md` are frontmatter-FREE; the renderer injects per-host frontmatter [source: command/AGENTS.md:20-22, bin/create-loop.js:278].
- `render` must be byte-for-byte deterministic on unchanged `command/` â€” enforced by the installer test [source: command/AGENTS.md:15, test/installer.test.js:81-91].
- Any change to a `command/` body or `manifest.json` must be followed by re-render and committing `command/` AND both rendered dirs together [source: command/AGENTS.md:12-16].

## Conventions

- The edit â†’ render â†’ commit sequence is mandatory: (1) edit `command/<id>.md` and/or `manifest.json`; (2) `node bin/create-loop.js render`; (3) `node test/installer.test.js`; (4) commit `command/` + `.opencode/command/` + `.claude/commands/` together [source: command/AGENTS.md:12-16].
- Adding a command: add `command/loop-foo.md` (no frontmatter) â†’ add a `manifest.json` entry (`id`, `description`, optional `argumentHint`, `"body":"loop-foo.md"`) â†’ render â†’ test â†’ commit all three dirs [source: command/AGENTS.md:23-24].
- Rendered filename is `<id>.md`; the `id` becomes the slash command name [source: bin/create-loop.js:331, 441].
- `command/manifest.schema.json` (Draft-07) validates the manifest: `version` const 1, `id` matches `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$`, `body` ends `.md`, `additionalProperties:false` [source: command/AGENTS.md:8].

## Common Mistakes

- Putting frontmatter in a `command/<id>.md` body â€” the renderer owns frontmatter; storing it in the body would double it and let the two hosts drift [source: command/AGENTS.md:19-22].
- Editing a rendered file under `.opencode/command/` or `.claude/commands/` â€” it is overwritten on every `render` [source: command/AGENTS.md:21].
- Committing a `command/` change without re-rendering â€” host files drift silently from source [source: AGENTS.md:51].

## Verified Facts

- Four commands are declared, each mapping to a skill mode: `loop-new` (Mode A, arg `<goal text>`), `loop-run` (Mode B, arg `[node-id]`), `loop-resume` (Mode C, arg `[loop-dir]`), `loop-status` (read-only, arg `[loop-id]`) [source: command/manifest.json:5-30].
- OpenCode frontmatter injected: `description` + `agent: build` [source: bin/create-loop.js:92-96].
- Claude Code frontmatter injected: `description` + `argument-hint` (only when `argumentHint` is set) [source: bin/create-loop.js:113-119].
- The manifest is versioned `version: 1` [source: command/manifest.json:3].
- Rendered dirs are regenerated by `cmdRender` targeting `.opencode/command` and `.claude/commands` at the repo root [source: bin/create-loop.js:431-434].
- Leading whitespace is stripped from each loaded body before rendering [source: bin/create-loop.js:278].

## Working Understanding

- Storing the body once and rendering frontmatter per host is the stated mechanism for keeping OpenCode and Claude command files from diverging [source: command/README.md, command/AGENTS.md:20-22]. This is why the two rendered files share an identical body from line 6 onward.
- `npx skills add` never writes a host command dir, which is why commands ship at the repo root and are installed by a separate path [source: README.md:140-143].
